steps.title: Find the perimeter of square S
steps.content:
Square R has side length $x$, so its perimeter is $4x$.

Square S's perimeter is 28 inches greater than square R's perimeter:
$$\text{Perimeter of S} = 4x + 28$$

steps.title: Determine the side length of square S
steps.content:
The side length of square S equals its perimeter divided by 4:
$$\text{Side of S} = \frac{4x + 28}{4} = \frac{4(x + 7)}{4} = x + 7$$

steps.title: Express the area function
steps.content:
The area of square S is the square of its side length:
$$f(x) = (x + 7)^2$$
That confirms A is correct.
